title: Universal Health Services, Inc. (UHS) CEO Marc Miller on Q2 2022 Results -Earnings Call Transcript
text: Universal Health Services, Inc. (NYSE:UHS ) Q2 2022 Earnings Conference Call July 26, 2022 9:00 AM ET Company Participants Steve Filton – Chief Financial Officer Marc Miller – President and Chief Executive Officer Conference Call Participants Marco Criscuolo – Nephron Research Andrew Mok – UBS Stephen Baxter – Wells Fargo Benjamin Rossi – BMO Jason Cassorla – Citi Whit Mayo – SVB Securities A. J. Rice – Credit Suisse Ben Hendrix – RBC Capital Markets Kevin Fischbeck – Bank of America Pito Chickering – Deutsche Bank Operator Good morning, and thank you for standing by.

title: Universal Health (UHS) Q2 Earnings Beat on Strong Segments
text: Universal Health's (UHS) second-quarter results benefit from strong contributions from Acute Care Hospital Services and Behavioral Health Care Services segments, partly offset by elevated operating costs.

title: Universal Health Services (UHS) Beats Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Universal Health Services (UHS) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 5.77% and 0.08%, respectively, for the quarter ended June 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Want to Get Richer? Invest in These Stocks and Wait 10 Years.
text: REITs can help you win the wealth game with little effort.

title: Here's How Universal Health (UHS) Looks Ahead of Q2 Earnings
text: Universal Health's (UHS) second-quarter results are likely to benefit from sound contributions from Acute Care Hospital Services segment. Increased labor costs might partly hurt results.

title: UNIVERSAL HEALTH SERVICES, INC. ANNOUNCES DATE FOR SECOND QUARTER 2022 EARNINGS RELEASE AND CONFERENCE CALL
text: KING OF PRUSSIA, Pa. , July 14, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Universal Health Services, Inc. (NYSE: UHS) announced today that it will report results for its second quarter ended June 30, 2022, after the market closes on Monday, July 25, 2022.

title: Universal Health (UHS) Cuts Guidance, Falls 6.1%: What's Ahead?
text: Universal Health's (UHS) acute care hospitals experience lower-than-expected patient volumes in the second quarter, inducing a decline in revenues and profits.
